This agreement governs the relationship between the parties with respect to the provision of consulting services over the duration of the engagement period. The parties acknowledge that the scope of work may evolve over time and agree to document any material changes through mutually executed change orders or amendments attached to this agreement as schedules. Both parties are committed to maintaining open communication throughout the project lifecycle and to escalating any concerns through the designated project managers before pursuing formal dispute resolution mechanisms. The consulting firm agrees to assign qualified personnel with the necessary expertise to fulfill the obligations set out in the applicable statement of work and to replace personnel promptly upon reasonable written request from the client where performance concerns have been documented. Client acknowledges that the consulting firm may utilize subcontractors or independent contractors to fulfill portions of the services, provided that such subcontractors are bound by confidentiality and intellectual property obligations no less protective than those set forth in this agreement. The client shall provide timely access to its systems, data, personnel, and facilities as reasonably necessary for the consulting firm to perform the services. Any delay in client-side deliverables may extend the project timeline by a corresponding period without liability to the consulting firm. The parties agree to meet at least monthly during the engagement to review progress, address open issues, and confirm alignment on priorities going forward.

The payment structure under this agreement is designed to reflect the milestone-based nature of the engagement while ensuring that the consulting firm is compensated for work in progress. The client shall pay a non-refundable retainer upon execution of this agreement, which will be applied against the final invoice. Milestone payments are due within net 60 days of the client's written acceptance of each deliverable, provided that acceptance shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed beyond ten business days of delivery. In the event that the client fails to provide written feedback within the feedback window, the deliverable shall be deemed accepted and the corresponding milestone payment shall become due and payable immediately. Late payments shall accrue interest at the statutory rate from the due date until the date of actual payment. The consulting firm reserves the right to suspend services if any undisputed invoice remains unpaid for more than thirty days following written notice of the overdue amount, and such suspension shall not constitute a breach of this agreement or give rise to any right of termination by the client.

All confidential information exchanged between the parties in connection with this engagement shall be protected with the same degree of care that each party uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care. Confidential information includes technical data, business plans, customer lists, pricing information, software code, and any other information identified as confidential at the time of disclosure or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure. The receiving party shall use the confidential information solely for the purpose of performing its obligations or exercising its rights under this agreement. The obligations of confidentiality shall not apply to information that is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the receiving party, was already known to the receiving party prior to disclosure, is independently developed by the receiving party without use of the disclosing party's confidential information, or is required to be disclosed by applicable law or court order, provided that the receiving party gives the disclosing party prompt written notice and cooperates with any effort to obtain a protective order.

The intellectual property provisions of this agreement are intended to strike a fair balance between the consulting firm's need to retain ownership of its general methodologies, tools, and pre-existing work product, and the client's legitimate expectation that it will own the custom deliverables produced specifically for this engagement. Accordingly, the parties agree that all deliverables created by the consulting firm exclusively for the client and fully paid for under this agreement shall be assigned to the client upon receipt of final payment. The consulting firm retains a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual license to use any general methodologies, frameworks, and non-client-specific know-how developed or refined during the engagement. The client represents and warrants that any materials, data, or software provided to the consulting firm for use in the engagement do not infringe the intellectual property rights of any third party, and the client shall indemnify the consulting firm against any claims arising from such infringement.
